rows.yaml:
collection: General
playbooks:
  - name: Approve or Reject Gate
    description: >
      Manual trigger that pauses on an analyst decision step.
      The analyst picks Approve or Reject; each path stamps a verdict
      variable and terminates cleanly.
    is_active: true
    steps:
      - type: start
        name: Start
        next: Analyst Decision

      - type: manual_input
        name: Analyst Decision
        arguments:
          title: "Review & Decide"
          description: >
            Please review the details and choose an action.
            Your selection will stamp the final verdict for this workflow run.
        options:
          - display: Approve
            primary: true
            next: Stamp Approved
          - display: Reject
            next: Stamp Rejected

      - type: set_variable
        name: Stamp Approved
        vars:
          verdict: approved
          verdict_note: "Analyst approved this request."
        message:
          content: "✅ Verdict stamped: **Approved** by the responding analyst."
          tags:
            - approved
            - manual_decision
        next: End Approved

      - type: set_variable
        name: Stamp Rejected
        vars:
          verdict: rejected
          verdict_note: "Analyst rejected this request."
        message:
          content: "❌ Verdict stamped: **Rejected** by the responding analyst."
          tags:
            - rejected
            - manual_decision
        next: End Rejected

      - type: end
        name: End Approved

      - type: end
        name: End Rejected

matches_example.detail: compiled JSON differs from the reference example

rows.yaml:
collection: SOC Playbooks
playbooks:
  - name: Confirm Before Block
    description: >
      Presents a manual approval gate to the SOC analyst before executing
      a block action. Approve routes to a confirmation step; Reject routes
      to a cancellation step.
    is_active: true
    steps:
      - type: start
        name: Start
        next: Confirm Block Decision

      - type: manual_input
        name: Confirm Block Decision
        arguments:
          title: "Approve Block Action?"
          description: >
            A block action has been requested. Review the details and decide
            whether to proceed. Select **Approve** to confirm the block or
            **Reject** to cancel.
          inputs:
            - name: analyst_comment
              kind: textarea
              label: Analyst Comment
              tooltip: "Optional: add context for the audit trail."
              required: false
        options:
          - display: Approve
            primary: true
            next: Record Approved Block
          - display: Reject
            next: Record Rejected Block

      - type: set_variable
        name: Record Approved Block
        vars:
          block_decision: "approved"
          decision_comment: "{{ vars.steps.Confirm_Block_Decision.input.analyst_comment }}"
        message:
          content: >
            ✅ Block action **approved** by analyst.
            Comment: {{ vars.steps.Confirm_Block_Decision.input.analyst_comment | default('(none)') }}
          tags:
            - block_approved
            - soc_action
        next: End Approved

      - type: set_variable
        name: Record Rejected Block
        vars:
          block_decision: "rejected"
          decision_comment: "{{ vars.steps.Confirm_Block_Decision.input.analyst_comment }}"
        message:
          content: >
            ❌ Block action **rejected** by analyst.
            Comment: {{ vars.steps.Confirm_Block_Decision.input.analyst_comment | default('(none)') }}
          tags:
            - block_rejected
            - soc_action
        next: End Rejected

      - type: end
        name: End Approved

      - type: end
        name: End Rejected

## ⚠️ Connector Not Found: `totally-made-up-connector`

The connector **`totally-made-up-connector`** does not exist in the FortiSOAR connector reference store. No operation `do_thing` can be resolved either. Authoring a playbook against an unknown connector would produce a broken, unrunnable artefact — so I'm stopping here rather than shipping bad YAML.

### What you can do

| Option | How |
|---|---|
| **Check the exact name** | In FortiSOAR, go to **Automation → Connectors** and copy the connector's exact slug (e.g. `virustotal`, `servicenow`, `fortigate`). |
| **Search by vendor or keyword** | Tell me the *vendor or product name* (e.g. "Palo Alto XSOAR", "CrowdStrike Falcon") and I'll fuzzy-search the catalog for you. |
| **Check if it's installed** | I can call `list_configured_connectors` to see which connectors are actually active on your FSR instance right now. |
| **Use a different connector** | If you meant one of `zscaler-client-connector`, `otbase-inventory`, or `nmap-scanner` (the nearest fuzzy matches), let me know and I'll look up their available operations. |

Once you confirm the correct connector name, I'll look up its operations, build the playbook, and run it through the full validation gate before showing you any YAML.

rows.yaml:
collection: SOC Playbooks
playbooks:
  - name: FortiGate IP Block or Allow
    description: >
      Prompts an analyst for an IP address and an action (block/allow).
      On 'block', quarantine-blocks the IP via the FortiGate connector.
      On 'allow', logs the decision as a workflow variable note.
    is_active: true
    steps:
      - type: start
        name: Start
        next: Analyst IP Action

      - type: manual_input
        name: Analyst IP Action
        arguments:
          title: "IP Address Action"
          description: >
            Enter the target IP address and choose whether to **block** it
            on the FortiGate firewall or **allow** it (no firewall change).
          inputs:
            - name: target_ip
              kind: ipv4
              label: Target IP Address
              required: true
              tooltip: "IPv4 address to block or allow (e.g. 192.168.1.100)"
            - name: action
              kind: select
              label: Action
              required: true
              options:
                - block
                - allow
        options:
          - display: Submit
            primary: true
            next: Check Action

      - type: decision
        name: Check Action
        conditions:
          - display: Block
            when: "{{ vars.steps.Analyst_IP_Action.input.action == 'block' }}"
            next: Block IP on FortiGate
          - display: Allow
            default: true
            next: Log Allow Decision

      - type: connector
        name: Block IP on FortiGate
        arguments:
          connector: fortigate-firewall
          operation: block_ip_new
          config: ''
          params:
            method: Quarantine Based
            ip_addresses: "{{ vars.steps.Analyst_IP_Action.input.target_ip }}"
            time_to_live: "12 Hour"
        next: Log Block Result

      - type: set_variable
        name: Log Block Result
        vars:
          block_status: >-
            {{ vars.steps.Block_IP_on_FortiGate.newly_blocked
               | default([]) | length }} IP(s) newly blocked;
            {{ vars.steps.Block_IP_on_FortiGate.already_blocked
               | default([]) | length }} already blocked.
          actioned_ip: "{{ vars.steps.Analyst_IP_Action.input.target_ip }}"
        message:
          content: >-
            ✅ FortiGate block submitted for **{{ vars.steps.Analyst_IP_Action.input.target_ip }}**.
            Result — newly blocked: {{ vars.steps.Block_IP_on_FortiGate.newly_blocked | default([]) }},
            already blocked: {{ vars.steps.Block_IP_on_FortiGate.already_blocked | default([]) }},
            errors: {{ vars.steps.Block_IP_on_FortiGate.error_with_block | default([]) }}.
          tags:
            - fortigate
            - ip_block
            - automation
        next: End Block

      - type: set_variable
        name: Log Allow Decision
        vars:
          allow_note: >-
            Analyst chose ALLOW for {{ vars.steps.Analyst_IP_Action.input.target_ip }}.
            No firewall change applied.
          actioned_ip: "{{ vars.steps.Analyst_IP_Action.input.target_ip }}"
        message:
          content: >-
            ℹ️ Analyst chose **allow** for IP **{{ vars.steps.Analyst_IP_Action.input.target_ip }}**.
            No FortiGate block was applied.
          tags:
            - fortigate
            - ip_allow
            - analyst_decision
        next: End Allow

      - type: end
        name: End Block

      - type: end
        name: End Allow

collection: Phishing Response
playbooks:
  - name: Phishing Alert IP Reputation Check and Block
    description: >
      On phishing-tagged alert creation, score every sender IP via VirusTotal.
      If any IP scores above 50, ask an analyst to Block or Skip.
      On Block, quarantine the high-score IPs on FortiGate.
      Always finish by posting a summary comment on the originating alert.
    is_active: true
    steps:

      # ── Trigger ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
      - type: start_on_create
        name: Start
        arguments:
          module: alerts
          when:
            logic: AND
            filters:
              - field: name
                op: like
                value: phishing
        next: Init Vars

      # ── Seed accumulators ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
      - type: set_variable
        name: Init Vars
        vars:
          high_score_ips: []
          action_taken: Skip
        next: Score All Sender IPs

      # ── Per-IP VirusTotal lookup (sequential) ─────────────────────────────
      - type: connector
        name: Score All Sender IPs
        for_each:
          item: "{{ vars.input.records[0].sender_ips }}"
          parallel: false
        arguments:
          connector: virustotal
          operation: query_ip
          config: ''
          params:
            ip: "{{ vars.item }}"
        next: Collect High Score IPs

      # ── Filter down to IPs whose reputation score > 50 ───────────────────
      - type: set_variable
        name: Collect High Score IPs
        vars:
          high_score_ips: >-
            {{ vars.steps.Score_All_Sender_IPs
               | selectattr('reputation', 'defined')
               | selectattr('reputation', 'ne', None)
               | map('dicttransform', {'ip': 'ip', 'score': 'reputation'})
               | selectattr('score', 'greaterthan', 50)
               | map(attribute='ip')
               | list }}
        next: Any High Score IP

      # ── Gate: did any IP exceed the threshold? ────────────────────────────
      - type: decision
        name: Any High Score IP
        conditions:
          - display: Score Above 50
            when: "{{ vars.high_score_ips | length > 0 }}"
            next: Analyst Block Decision
          - display: All Clear
            default: true
            next: Summarize And Comment

      # ── Analyst prompt ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
      - type: manual_input
        name: Analyst Block Decision
        arguments:
          title: High-Score Sender IPs Detected
          description: >-
            One or more sender IPs exceeded a VirusTotal reputation score of 50.
            High-score IPs: {{ vars.high_score_ips | join(', ') }}.
            Please choose an action.
        options:
          - display: Block
            primary: true
            next: Was Block Chosen
          - display: Skip
            next: Was Block Chosen

      # ── Branch on analyst choice ──────────────────────────────────────────
      - type: decision
        name: Was Block Chosen
        conditions:
          - display: Block
            when: "{{ vars.steps.Analyst_Block_Decision.input.analyst_choice == 'Block' }}"
            next: Block High Score IPs
          - display: Skip
            default: true
            next: Summarize And Comment

      # ── Quarantine each high-score IP on FortiGate ────────────────────────
      - type: connector
        name: Block High Score IPs
        for_each:
          item: "{{ vars.high_score_ips }}"
          parallel: false
        arguments:
          connector: fortigate-firewall
          operation: block_ip_new
          config: ''
          params:
            method: Quarantine Based
            ip_addresses: "{{ vars.item }}"
            time_to_live: 1 Day
        next: Set Action Taken

      # ── Record that we actually blocked ───────────────────────────────────
      - type: set_variable
        name: Set Action Taken
        vars:
          action_taken: Block
        next: Summarize And Comment

      # ── Post summary comment on the originating alert ─────────────────────
      - type: set_variable
        name: Summarize And Comment
        message:
          content: |
            **Phishing Alert — Sender IP Reputation Summary**

            | IP | VT Reputation Score | High Score? |
            |---|---|---|
            {% for result in vars.steps.Score_All_Sender_IPs %}
            | {{ result.ip }} | {{ result.reputation | default('N/A') }} | {{ 'Yes' if (result.reputation | int(0)) > 50 else 'No' }} |
            {% endfor %}

            **High-score IPs (>50):** {{ vars.high_score_ips | join(', ') | default('None', true) }}
            **Action taken:** {{ vars.action_taken }}
        next: End

      - type: end
        name: End
